title: TC001-Homepage renders core UI sections
description: Verifies the homepage shows the search bar and key discovery sections for browsing local goods.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//input[@placeholder="Search" or @aria-label="Search" or contains(@id,"search")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Categories').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Regional').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Products').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Tap a featured category shows category product listing
description: Verifies selecting a featured category leads to a product listing view for that category.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Search the page for the text 'Categories' (fall back to 'Kategori' if not found), then click the first featured category tile (the first category button, index 69).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/main/div[2]/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the first visible featured category tile (button index 807) to open the product listing for that category, then verify the product listing view renders.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/main/div[2]/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Wait briefly for any navigation/rendering after the click
        await page.wait_for_timeout(1000)
        # Assert the categories control ("Semua 6") is visible
        cat_ctrl = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div[1]/div/div[3]/main/div[2]/div[2]/button[1]')
        assert await cat_ctrl.is_visible(), 'Categories control (Semua 6) is not visible'
        # Assert the product listing header/text (contains "Produk") is visible
        prod_header = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div[1]/div/div[3]/main/div[4]/a[1]')
        assert await prod_header.is_visible(), 'Products text (product listing header) is not visible'
        # Assert a product card is visible in the listing
        prod_card = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div[1]/div/div[3]/main/div[4]/a[2]')
        assert await prod_card.is_visible(), 'Product card is not visible'
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Open a product from homepage listing navigates to product detail URL
description: Verifies tapping a product card from the homepage leads to the product detail page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Product card').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/product/' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Product details').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- No interactive product card elements found on the homepage, preventing a tap/click action to reach a product detail page.
- Product area displays only skeleton placeholders, indicating product items either failed to load or the product click feature is not implemented.
- Cannot verify navigation to '/product/' because no clickable product card exists on the page.

title: TC007-Search for a valid keyword returns matching product results
description: Verifies that entering a keyword and running search shows a visible results list.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /search (http://localhost:5174/search) as the explicit test step requires.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/search", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type 'beras' into the search input (element index 63) then click the search button (element index 69).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('beras')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Search').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Results').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//ul[@id="search-results" or contains(@class,"search-results") or contains(@class,"results-list")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Search results list element not visible on page after running search for 'beras'.
- Page displays message 'Tidak ada hasil yang ditemukan' indicating no product results were returned for the search.

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /seller/abc (explicit navigation per test step).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/seller/abc", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Product Card').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/product/' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Product Details').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC030-Browse categories from Etalase landing page
description: Verifies the catalog page loads and shows a visible list of categories for browsing.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /etalase (http://localhost:5174/etalase) using the navigate action.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/etalase", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert "/etalase" in frame.url
        locator = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[1]/div/div[2]/div/div/div/a[2]')
        assert await locator.is_visible()
        text = await locator.inner_text()
        assert "Etalase" in text
        raise AssertionError("Element with text 'Kategori' not found in the available elements; cannot verify the category header. Task marked done.")
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
